theory.intro: A fraction only makes sense when you know the whole it refers to. Two fractions can only be compared or added when they describe the **same whole**.
facts: $\tfrac{1}{2}$ of a small pizza is **less** than $\tfrac{1}{2}$ of a large pizza — the wholes differ.
To compare or add fractions, the **whole must be the same size**.
The denominator names how many equal parts the **one whole** is split into.
steps: Identify the whole each fraction refers to.
Check the wholes are the same size.
Only then compare, add or subtract the fractions.
